title: Collection and Post-Surgical Excision of Human Kidney Tissue
author: Maya Brewer
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.buggnttw
materials: Receive excised kidney from full nephrectomy.

steps.title: Kidney tissues are selected based on...
steps.instruction: Kidney tissues are selected based on patient and clinical metadata (e.g. age<65 years old). All patients have consented to allow their tissue to be used for research purposes as part of this study.

steps.title: Cut kidney in half along the...
steps.instruction: Cut kidney in half along the longest plane and open like a book.

steps.title: Cut out a portion of the...
steps.instruction: Cut out a portion of the kidney (~80 x 60 x 4 mm) that is farthest away from the tumor location.

steps.title: Take a picture of the smaller...
steps.instruction: Take a picture of the smaller piece of kidney in reference to its original location within the entire kidney if possible.

steps.title: Cut the piece of kidney into...
steps.instruction: Cut the piece of kidney into ~4 smaller, rectangular pieces (~2 x 15 x 4 mm).

steps.title: Image each of these smaller pieces...
steps.instruction: Image each of these smaller pieces in reference to their original position.

steps.title: Place tissue in plastic container on ice
steps.instruction: Place tissue in plastic container on ice. Transfer tissue to those preparing tissue for long-term storage.
